items.title: The SEC wants to let newly public companies raise cash instantly in its biggest rule change in decades
items.summary: The agency is proposing its largest overhaul of public listing rules in over 20 years, cutting compliance costs and giving crypto firms a much easier path to raise cash on Wall Street.

items.title: People Are More Willing to Lie to AI Than Humans, Study Finds
items.summary: Consumers feel less social pressure when lying to an AI chatbot, research finds, while human-like social cues reduce dishonest behavior.

items.title: The U.S. can’t lose the bitcoin race to China
items.summary: The next global power competition is not being fought over missiles alone. It’s being fought over money, and right now, China is moving aggressively to shape the future of it, argues Gooden.

items.title: Canaan Shares Plunge as CEO Says Middle East Conflict Is Clouding Outlook for Bitcoin Miners
items.summary: Canaan shares fell after the Bitcoin mining company disclosed an $88.7 million first-quarter net loss, its second straight negative period.

items.title: Bitcoin price stays under $77K as US bond yields near 20-year highs
items.summary: BTC price stayed pinned below $77,000 amid rising US bond yields and oil prices, with market analysts saying Bitcoin is now at a "crucial level of support."

items.title: A viral hedgehog, Vitalik Buterin, and a bow: the GraphDex launch that crypto won't forget
items.summary: 5,800 users in two hours. One QR code. One bow that went viral.

items.title: Tor Project to lead Web3 crowdfunding to support internet freedom
items.summary: Global internet freedom has declined for 15 consecutive years, with more countries restricting access to more of the internet, according to Freedom House.

items.title: ‘What's happening at the EF?’ Ethereum community looking for answers after high-profile departures
items.summary: A wave of recent high-profile departures at the Ethereum Foundation has reignited longstanding questions from community members about what is going on inside the organization.

items.title: Former BNY exec launches NUVA, bets tokenization will remake Wall Street
items.summary: NUVA launched this week with nearly $19 billion in tokenized real-world assets from Figure Technologies, aiming to bring regulated U.S. yield products into DeFi.

items.title: Bitcoin Giant Strategy Gets Price Target Boost From TD Cowen After $2 Billion BTC Buy
items.summary: Analysts at TD Cowen believe shares in Bitcoin-buying Strategy behemoth could jump more than 139% in the next 12 months.

items.title: Senator Elizabeth Warren accuses U.S. regulator of approving unqualified crypto banks
items.summary: The Senate Banking Committee's top Democrat sent a letter to the Office of the Comptroller of the Currency questioning the charters of nine crypto firms.

items.title: 21Shares says Hyperliquid ETF demand shows appetite for 24/7 trading
items.summary: 21Shares says strong early flows into its new Hyperliquid ETF reflect growing investor demand for around-the-clock access to crypto and traditional assets.

items.title: Polymarket unlocks $5 trillion private market for retail traders, previously reserved for elites
items.summary: Polymarket’s new private-company prediction markets let retail traders bet on startup milestones once reserved for Wall Street insiders.

items.title: Crypto IPO boom stalls as AI frenzy reshapes tech markets, says Fundstrat exec
items.summary: Crypto firms are pausing long-awaited IPO plans as weak trading volumes and macro pressures weigh on valuations despite boom in AI-linked tech listings.

items.title: Japan’s Ruling Party Pushes On-Chain Finance Plan to Protect Yen
items.summary: Stablecoins and tokenized deposits could help Japan modernize payments and reduce reliance on foreign rails, the proposal says.

items.title: Recent Bitcoin holders sell $770M BTC at a loss amid $65K BTC price calls
items.summary: More than 10,000 Bitcoin have been sold at a loss by short-term holders in recent days, adding fuel to analysts’ predictions that BTC price will fall to $65,000.

items.title: Live markets: Bitcoin gives up $77,000 as interest rates continue surge, stocks continue slump
items.summary: Crypto markets have turned lower in morning U.S. trade on Tuesday as the idea of coming rate hikes takes a toll on risk assets.

items.title: Bitcoin miner Canaan posts $88.7M net loss in Q1 amid BTC decline
items.summary: Canaan’s Q1 results were dragged down by a $25 million inventory write-down and a 75% quarterly drop in equipment sales as Bitcoin prices retreated from their highs.

items.title: JPMorgan says ether and altcoins won't catch up to bitcoin without a major network boom
items.summary: The bank said ether and the broader altcoin market continue to trail bitcoin as weak network activity, sluggish DeFi growth and limited real-world adoption weigh on investor demand.

items.title: AI Slop Floods Bug Bounty Programs as Companies Struggle with Fake Reports
items.summary: Bug bounty platforms and software companies are struggling to handle a surge of low-quality, AI-generated vulnerability reports.

items.title: Estonian regulator partially suspends Zondacrypto operator license
items.summary: Estonia’s FIU has partially suspended BB Trade Estonia OÜ’s license, giving the Zondacrypto operator 30 days to fix compliance issues or risk full revocation.

items.title: Zcash is 'running its own bull market' as ZEC price paints 88% rally setup

items.title: South Korean funeral company records $33M unrealized loss on leveraged ETH ETFs
